skill.name: ai-research-explore
skill.description: Coordinate candidate-only deep learning research exploration from a fixed current_research anchor, with source lookup, idea ranking, bounded execution planning, and auditable outputs.
skill.summary: Plan and audit bounded deep learning research candidates from a fixed research anchor.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical heuristic combinations from command execution, network, credentials, filesystem, and hashing patterns, but review did not confirm malicious intent or prompt injection. Most backtick, path traversal, and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown, artifact text, and non-security stable hashes. The remaining real risks are legitimate but sensitive: arbitrary URL metadata lookup and explicit experiment command execution require user review and warnings.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Research URL Fetching
medium_findings.description: The lookup pass can resolve generic HTTP and HTTPS locators from campaign inputs and repo-local extracted references, then fetch those URLs for metadata. This is legitimate for research source lookup, but it can contact arbitrary hosts if untrusted repositories or campaign files are used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network fetch path is clear and can use arbitrary generic URLs. The risk is bounded to metadata lookup and has no confirmed credential exfiltration, so medium severity is appropriate.

medium_findings.title: Explicit Experiment Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The orchestrator can compose experiment commands from a variant matrix and pass them to external run helpers when selected variant execution is enabled. This matches the skill purpose, but unsafe campaign input could execute undesired local commands in a user repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are delegated through Python argument lists and require explicit execution mode, but the command string itself comes from experiment planning inputs.

low_findings.title: Optional Provider Environment Checks
low_findings.description: The optional provider module names API key environment variables and checks whether they exist. I did not find evidence that the values are read into records, logged, or sent over the network in this placeholder adapter.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected code only checks truthiness with os.environ.get and immediately returns None. There is no observed use of the secret values.

low_findings.title: Non-Security Hashes Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Multiple weak cryptography findings are false positives because SHA-1 is used for stable identifiers, cache keys, context IDs, and filenames. I did not find evidence that these hashes protect secrets, passwords, signatures, or integrity boundaries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant code creates deterministic IDs and artifact names from local metadata. That semantic context makes the weak-crypto alert highly likely to be a false positive.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick execution findings are false positives from Markdown inline code, generated explanatory strings, or artifact text. These occurrences describe fields, paths, metrics, and commands rather than executing shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown or f-string output text and are not Ruby code. Real subprocess use is evaluated separately.

low_findings.title: Relative Reference Paths Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Path traversal alerts in SKILL.md refer to relative skill reference files such as ../../references/agent-operating-principles.md. They are documentation loading instructions, not runtime filesystem traversal with attacker-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are fixed documentation references in the skill manual. No dynamic file read or write based on these strings was observed at those locations.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review for phrases such as ignore previous instructions, system update, pre-approved, verified safe, and elevated privileges found no prompt injection attempt in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The check covered the primary skill instruction file and policy references. It cannot prove absence in every line, but no evidence was found in reviewed content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Research URL Fetching
dangerous_patterns.description: The lookup pass can resolve generic HTTP and HTTPS locators from campaign inputs and repo-local extracted references, then fetch those URLs for metadata. This is legitimate for research source lookup, but it can contact arbitrary hosts if untrusted repositories or campaign files are used.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network fetch path is clear and can use arbitrary generic URLs. The risk is bounded to metadata lookup and has no confirmed credential exfiltration, so medium severity is appropriate.

dangerous_patterns.title: Explicit Experiment Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The orchestrator can compose experiment commands from a variant matrix and pass them to external run helpers when selected variant execution is enabled. This matches the skill purpose, but unsafe campaign input could execute undesired local commands in a user repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are delegated through Python argument lists and require explicit execution mode, but the command string itself comes from experiment planning inputs.

content.value_statement: Research exploration can drift into weak comparisons, broad rewrites, or unclear evidence. This skill structures candidate ideas, source support, execution gates, and audit artifacts around a fixed research anchor.
actual_capabilities: Validates that exploration starts from a durable current_research anchor.
Creates isolated experiment branch or worktree metadata for candidate work.
Collects and caches source records from seed sources, repository references, arXiv, DOI, GitHub, and web URLs.
Ranks candidate ideas using evidence, cost, risk, rollback ease, and evaluation fit.
Builds auditable artifacts such as idea cards, comparison notes, source support, and experiment manifests.
Can delegate selected short-run experiments to existing run helpers when execution is explicitly enabled.
limitations: It does not prove novelty, SOTA superiority, or global literature completeness.
It requires a clear task, dataset, benchmark, evaluation source, and current_research anchor.
Network lookup can fail or return incomplete metadata when providers are unavailable.
Execution remains bounded and depends on the target repository and installed helper skills.

use_cases.title: Rank Candidate Research Ideas
use_cases.description: Compare several candidate changes against a frozen dataset, benchmark, and evaluation contract before spending compute.

use_cases.title: Audit Source-Backed Adaptations
use_cases.description: Map papers, repositories, and implementation hints into reversible patch plans with clear source support.

use_cases.title: Govern Exploratory Runs
use_cases.description: Separate trusted reproduction work from exploratory variants and require evidence before scaling experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bounded Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a clear research anchor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore with current_research=<branch or commit>. Task family: <task>. Dataset: <dataset>. Benchmark: <benchmark>. Evaluation source: <path or command>. SOTA reference: <citation or metric>. Plan candidate-only exploration and do not run experiments yet.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Provided Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore to rank these candidate ideas against current_research=<anchor>. Preserve the provided evaluation source and SOTA reference. Score expected gain, cost, evaluation risk, implementation risk, and rollback ease.

prompt_templates.title: Map Sources to Patch Plans
prompt_templates.scenario: Connect literature or repository sources to feasible changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore with the attached research_campaign. Resolve source support from seed sources and repo-local references. Produce source mapping, idea cards, and minimal reversible patch plans only.

prompt_templates.title: Run One Selected Variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced execution after review and authorization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore with --run-selected-variants for one approved candidate. Use a short timeout, keep the experiment isolated, record metrics and changed files, and treat results as exploratory evidence only.

output_examples.input: A campaign with a current model checkpoint, CIFAR benchmark, evaluation script, and three candidate architecture changes.
output: A ranked idea list with evidence notes, cost estimates, and rollback guidance.
A source support summary that separates external provider records from seed-only records.
A checkpoint decision explaining whether to run, revise, or stop each candidate.

output_examples.input: A researcher asks whether a repository idea can be adapted without changing metric semantics.
output: A source mapping table with source files, target files, interface differences, and patch class.
A warning when the idea touches evaluation logic or requires broad train-loop changes.
A minimal plan for a reversible implementation and smoke validation.

output_examples.input: An approved short-run experiment for one candidate variant.
output: An experiment manifest with the selected idea, config overrides, planned files, and evidence references.
A comparability note that describes metric boundaries and SOTA comparison limits.
A run summary with command status, observed metrics, artifacts, and follow-up decision.

best_practices: Provide the current_research anchor, dataset, benchmark, evaluation source, SOTA reference, and compute budget before using the skill.
Review source support and idea ranking before allowing any exploratory command execution.
Keep each candidate single-variable, reversible, and separate from trusted reproduction work.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for open-ended brainstorming without a frozen research anchor.
Do not treat generated candidate gains as verified novelty or trusted reproduction success.
Do not enable network lookup or command execution in sensitive environments without reviewing inputs first.

faq.question: Does this skill prove a research idea is novel?
faq.answer: No. It treats novelty as a hypothesis until supported by literature contrast, ablations, and fair comparison.

faq.question: Can it run experiments automatically?
faq.answer: It can delegate one or more selected variants only when execution is explicitly enabled and helper scripts are available.

faq.question: What inputs are required?
faq.answer: It needs current_research, task family, dataset, benchmark, evaluation source, SOTA reference, and compute budget.

faq.question: Does it search the full literature?
faq.answer: No. It performs bounded source lookup from seeds, repository references, and supported public providers.

faq.question: Where are outputs written?
faq.answer: The workflow writes auditable records to analysis_outputs, sources, and explore_outputs when configured.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production model changes?
faq.answer: No. It is for candidate exploration and should feed a separate review, reproduction, and validation process.
